title: Hemigraphis dura
intro: Hemigraphis dura is a type of plant commonly known as the "tiger back plant" or "tiger grass", belonging to the family Acanthaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, and may be used to treat various health conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Hemigraphis dura include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Hemigraphis dura is often used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which are used to treat various health conditions, particularly those related to the respiratory and digestive systems.
taxonomy: Hemigraphis dura is a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae. It is native to parts of Asia, particularly in the tropical regions of India, Malaysia, and Thailand. The classification of Hemigraphis dura places it within the order Lamiales. This plant is often cultivated as an ornamental species for its attractive foliage and ability to thrive in humid environments. Hemigraphis dura is also known by its common name, Chinese dunia.
